initial_program:
% Count rows and associate with a dummy row index
total_rows(N,R) :- N = #count{R1 : input(R1, _, _)}.

% Column existence predicate
col_exists(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Count columns and associate with a dummy column index
total_cols(M,C,C1) :- M = #count{C1 : col_exists(C1)}.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column (keeping the row index of the counting)
col_nonzero(C,K,R1) :- K = #count{R1 : input(R1, C, V), V != 0}.

% For each nonzero input cell compute its target cell
source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2) :-
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    col_nonzero(C, K, R1),
    i = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R0},
    Rt = N - K + i.

% The colour at the target must be exactly V
:- source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2), output(Rt, C, W), color(W), W != V.

% Original cell of a moving nonzero must become zero
non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1) :-
    source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R1),
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    Rt != R0.

:- non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1), output(R0, C, W), color(W), W != 0.

% Zero cells that lie above the offset must be zero
zero_constraint(R, C, N, K, R1) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    col_nonzero(C, K, R1),
    R + K < N.

:- zero_constraint(R, C, N, K, R1), output(R, C, W), color(W), W != 0.

% Define all output cells (rows 0..N1, columns 0..M1)
output_cell(R, C, N, M) :-
    total_rows(N,R),
    total_cols(M, C, C1),
    R < N,
    C < M.

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C, N, M).

% Count rows and associate with a dummy row index
total_rows(N,R) :- N = #count{R1 : input(R1, _, _)}, R = N.

% Column existence predicate
col_exists(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Count columns and associate with a dummy column index
total_cols(M,C,C1) :- col_exists(C), col_exists(C1), M = #count{C1 : col_exists(C1)}.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column (keeping the row index of the counting)
col_nonzero(C,K,R1) :- col_exists(C), K = #count{R1 : input(R1, C, V), V != 0}.

% For each nonzero input cell compute its target cell
source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2) :-
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    col_nonzero(C, K, R1),
    i = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R0},
    Rt = N - K + i.

% The colour at the target must be exactly V
:- source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2), output(Rt, C, W), color(W), W != V.

% Original cell of a moving nonzero must become zero
non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1) :-
    source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R1),
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    Rt != R0.

:- non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1), output(R0, C, W), color(W), W != 0.

% Zero cells that lie above the offset must be zero
zero_constraint(R, C, N, K, R1) :- total_rows(N,R), input(R, C, 0), col_nonzero(C, K, R1), (R+K) < N.

:- zero_constraint(R, C, N, K, R1), output(R, C, W), color(W), W != 0.

% Define all output cells (rows 0..N1, columns 0..M1)
output_cell(R, C, N, M) :-
    total_rows(N,R),
    total_cols(M, C, C1),
    R < N,
    C < M.

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C, N, M).

% Count rows and associate with a dummy row index
total_rows(N,R) :- N = #count{R1 : input(R1, _, _)}, R = N.

% Column existence predicate
col_exists(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Count columns and associate with a dummy column index
total_cols(M,C,C1) :- col_exists(C), col_exists(C1), M = #count{C1 : col_exists(C1)}.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column (keeping the row index of the counting)
% col_nonzero predicate not needed

% For each nonzero input cell compute its target cell
source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2) :-
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    K = #count{R1 : input(R1, C, V), V != 0},
    i = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R0},
    Rt = N - K + i.

% The colour at the target must be exactly V
:- source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2), output(Rt, C, W), color(W), W != V.

% Original cell of a moving nonzero must become zero
non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1) :-
    source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R1),
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    Rt != R0.

:- non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1), output(R0, C, W), color(W), W != 0.

% Zero cells that lie above the offset must be zero
zero_constraint(R, C, N, K) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    col_exists(C),
    K = #count{R1 : input(R1, C, V), V != 0},
    R + K < N.
% Define all output cells (rows 0..N1, columns 0..M1)
output_cell(R, C, N, M) :-
    total_rows(N,R),
    total_cols(M, C, C1),
    R < N,
    C < M.

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C, N, M).

total_rows(N,R) :- N = #count{R1 : input(R1, _, _)}, R = N.

% Column existence predicate
col_exists(C) :- input(_, C, _).

% Count columns and associate with a dummy column index
total_cols(M,C,C1) :- col_exists(C), col_exists(C1), M = #count{C1 : col_exists(C1)}.

% Number of nonzero cells in a column (keeping the row index of the counting)
% col_nonzero predicate not needed

% For each nonzero input cell compute its target cell
source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2) :-
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    K = #count{R1 : input(R1, C, V), V != 0},
    i = #count{R2 : input(R2, C, V2), V2 != 0, R2 < R0},
    Rt = N - K + i.

% The colour at the target must be exactly V
:- source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R2), output(Rt, C, W), color(W), W != V.

% Original cell of a moving nonzero must become zero
non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1) :-
    source_target(Rt, C, V, N, K, i, R1),
    input(R0, C, V), V != 0,
    Rt != R0.

:- non_target_original(R0, C, Rt, R1), output(R0, C, W), color(W), W != 0.

% Zero cells that lie above the offset must be zero
zero_constraint(R, C, N, K) :-
    input(R, C, 0),
    col_exists(C),
    K = #count{R1 : input(R1, C, V), V != 0},
    R + K < N.
% Define all output cells (rows 0..N1, columns 0..M1)
output_cell(R, C, N, M) :-
    total_rows(N,R),
    total_cols(M, C, C1),
    R < N,
    C < M.

% Exactly one colour for each cell
1 { output(R, C, Color) : color(Color) } 1 :- output_cell(R, C, N, M).
